title: Microlepia calvescens
intro: Microlepia calvescens, also known as Gotu Kola, is a perennial plant species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Polypodiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its numerous health benefits, including improving cognitive function, enhancing wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Bioactive constituents of Gotu Kola include triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Gotu Kola is commonly prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is often used to support brain health, improve skin and hair conditions, and boost overall well-being.
taxonomy: Microlepia calvescens is classified as a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. More specifically, it belongs to the genus Microlepia, which is a group of ferns commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. This species is a member of the order Polypodiales, which comprises a wide range of ferns with varying characteristics. Microlepia calvescens is native to certain parts of Asia, where it grows in shaded, humid environments. The classification of Microlepia calvescens reflects its unique characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other ferns.
